records.ety: [OE. time, AS. tīma, akin to tīd time, and to Icel. tīmi, Dan. time an hour, Sw. timme. √58. See Tide, n.]

defs: Duration, considered independently of any system of measurement or any employment of terms which designate limited portions thereof.
quotes: The time wasteth [i. e. passes away] night and day.
I know of no ideas . . . that have a better claim to be accounted simple and original than those of space and time.

defs: A particular period or part of duration, whether past, present, or future; a point or portion of duration; as, the time was, or has been; the time is, or will be.
quotes: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets.

defs: The period at which any definite event occurred, or person lived; age; period; era; as, the Spanish Armada was destroyed in the time of Queen Elizabeth; -- often in the plural; as, ancient times; modern times.
The duration of one's life; the hours and days which a person has at his disposal.
quotes: Believe me, your time is not your own; it belongs to God, to religion, to mankind.

defs: A proper time; a season; an opportunity.
quotes: There is . . . a time to every purpose.
The time of figs was not yet.

defs: Hour of travail, delivery, or parturition.
quotes: She was within one month of her time.

defs: Performance or occurrence of an action or event, considered with reference to repetition; addition of a number to itself; repetition; as, to double cloth four times; four times four, or sixteen.
quotes: Summers three times eight save one.

defs: The present life; existence in this world as contrasted with immortal life; definite, as contrasted with infinite, duration.
quotes: Till time and sin together cease.

defs: The measured duration of sounds; measure; tempo; rate of movement; rhythmical division; as, common or triple time; the musician keeps good time.
quotes: Some few lines set unto a solemn time.
notes: ☞ Time is often used in the formation of compounds, mostly self-explaining; as, time-battered, time-beguiling, time-consecrated, time-consuming, time-enduring, time-killing, time-sanctioned, time-scorner, time-wasting, time-worn, etc.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Timed (tīmd); p. pr. & vb. n. Timing.]

defs: To appoint the time for; to bring, begin, or perform at the proper season or time; as, he timed his appearance rightly.
quotes: There is no greater wisdom than well to time the beginnings and onsets of things.

defs: To regulate as to time; to accompany, or agree with, in time of movement.
quotes: Who overlooked the oars, and timed the stroke.
He was a thing of blood, whose every motion Was timed with dying cries.

defs: To ascertain or record the time, duration, or rate of; as, to time the speed of horses, or hours for workmen.
To measure, as in music or harmony.
To keep or beat time; to proceed or move in time.
quotes: With oar strokes timing to their song.
